page: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 128 vCPUs, 64 physical cores, and hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 7559 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable. It does not include any GPU accelerators.

In benchmark evaluations, the D128ds_v7 exhibits top-tier multi-core performance. While its stress-ng single-core CPU performance is in the average tier at 2265.39, its multi-core CPU performance reaches 145450.5, placing it in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, with read-only small block cached speeds at 10740.63 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 658.72 GB/sec. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, contribute to top-tier scores in Passmark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests.

The server is highly optimized for data-intensive and concurrent workloads. It demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations, achieving 34561.98 in Passmark database benchmarks and 204.83 million operations per second in Redis SET operations. Static web serving throughput is also exceptional at 160.54 GB/sec. For machine learning, the CPU-based LLM inference shows strong prompt processing speeds, though text generation speeds remain in the average tier. This hardware profile offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale databases, heavy multi-threaded decompression tasks, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a high-capacity, general-purpose virtual machine powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It delivers 128 vCPUs, 64 physical cores, 512.0 GB of RAM, and a substantial 7559 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance excels in multi-threaded environments, demonstrating top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations. The integration of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512 instructions enables strong performance in cryptography and vector calculations. While single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average, its massive parallel processing capabilities and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for large-scale databases, high-throughput static web serving, and intensive multi-threaded data decompression.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 7559 GB of local storage, optimized for high-throughput database and multi-core workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a 128-vCPU virtual machine with 512 GB RAM and 7559 GB local storage, built for high-concurrency database workloads.
tagline: A high-density, 128-vCPU general-purpose instance with massive local storage, built for parallel processing and high-throughput database workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
512 GB of system memory paired with 7559 GB of local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 hardware acceleration for cryptography and vector tasks
Well-suited for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, and high-throughput web serving
